headline: Goldman Sachs Downgrades Intuit as AI Tax Rivals Intensify Competitive Pressure (INTU)
summary: Intuit (NASDAQ:INTU) came under pressure on Tuesday after Goldman Sachs downgraded the stock to Sell from Neutral and sharply reduced its 12-month price target to $276 from $519, citing mounting competitive risks in the tax software market that could weigh on the company’s long-term growth outlook. The brokerage said increasing competition from emerging AI-driven tax preparation platforms may limit Intuit’s ability to sustain current growth expectations and profitability levels.
